concepts.lesson: Food resources are obtained mainly from agriculture and animal husbandry. As the population increases, food production must also increase. Scientific farming methods and improved management practices help increase food production without damaging natural resources.
concepts.real_example: Farmers growing crops and raising livestock to supply food for people.
key_points: Food comes from plants and animals
Agriculture and animal husbandry are major sources

concepts.lesson: Crops are plants grown by farmers for food and other purposes. Cereals provide carbohydrates, pulses provide proteins, oil seeds provide fats, and fruits and vegetables provide vitamins and minerals.
concepts.real_example: Rice and wheat are cereals, gram and peas are pulses.
key_points: Fruits and vegetables give vitamins and minerals

concepts.lesson: Crop variety improvement is the process of developing new crop varieties with desirable traits such as higher yield, disease resistance, and improved quality. This can be achieved through hybridisation or genetic modification.
concepts.real_example: Developing wheat varieties that produce higher yield and resist diseases.

concepts.lesson: Crop production management refers to the practices used to increase crop yield. It includes nutrient management, irrigation and cropping patterns to ensure proper growth of crops.
concepts.real_example: Using fertilizers and irrigation systems to increase wheat production.

concepts.lesson: Plants require nutrients for growth and development. Nutrients are supplied through soil, water and air. Macronutrients are needed in large quantities, while micronutrients are required in smaller quantities.
concepts.real_example: Nitrogen and phosphorus added to soil using fertilizers.

concepts.lesson: Irrigation is the artificial supply of water to crops to support their growth. It helps crops survive during periods of low rainfall and ensures proper development of plants.
concepts.real_example: Water supplied to fields using wells, canals or tanks.

concepts.lesson: Cropping patterns refer to different ways of growing crops in fields to maximize production and reduce risks. Examples include mixed cropping, intercropping and crop rotation.
concepts.real_example: Growing wheat and gram together in the same field.

concepts.lesson: Crop protection management involves protecting crops from weeds, pests and diseases. Various methods such as pesticides, biological control and mechanical removal are used to prevent crop damage.
concepts.real_example: Using herbicides to remove weeds in crop fields.
key_points: Protects crops from pests and weeds

concepts.lesson: Animal husbandry is the scientific management of animals for food and other products. It includes feeding, breeding and disease control of livestock.
Fish production includes capture fishing and fish farming in marine or freshwater ecosystems. Bee-keeping is the maintenance of bee colonies to produce honey and wax.
concepts.real_example: Fish farming in ponds and honey production from beehives.
key_points: Fish obtained through capture or culture fisheries
